title: Due diligence audit
body:
We use the free version of excalidraw at work however I have recently advocated for the paid teams plan. In order to support this tool our company must conduct a due diligence audit such as data protection, ownership and infrastructure. I've had a look on the website's privacy policy but it doesn't seem to cover much around how data is stored for excalidraw plus.

I understand you use the Google Storage API / Firebase, but where physically is the data stored - i.e. what region of the world? It's important for my company to understand since this could be used to store sensitive customer information - although we don't explicitly plan on it, it's a whiteboard so these things can occur - so we must ensure that the security practises are compliant to the same standards we are (we're based in the UK)

title: How to run production build in development?
body:
So I'm trying to integrate excalidraw in our product. We are using React and importing the library as an ES6 module.

We have strict Content Security Policy (CSP). So allowing `unsafe-eval` is not an option for us.

As far as I can see, the development build of excalidraw is using `eval` (probably to inject source maps). The production build does not.

So if I manually edit `excalidraw/main.js` to use production build, everything works fine. but the change is not persisted

I am wondering if there is a more robust way to use the production build when importing as an ES6 module. Can you help?

Alternatively, removing `eval` from the development build should also help here. I don't know if that is an option.

Best,
Sami

title: using excalidraw on an "e-paper tablet" like remarkable2 or Onyx Boox Note Air 2
body:
Dear community

I was wondering if any of you have experience in using excalidraw and drawing with a stylus on any e-paper tablet, specifically one of the below. I guess you would run chrome on the tablet, open excalidraw.com, and then draw with the stylus.
- ~~remarkable 2~~ (so, this is not possible (duh), since it doesn't have a browser)
- Onyx Boox Note Air 2
- Onyx Boox Note 5
- or any older version or other tablet?

I am curious about how the hand-writing experience is:
- What is the lag? 
- How are the lines?
- How is the lag compared to using the "default" hand-writing app? (performance of chrome-excalidraw renderer)
- How is panning, zooming working (touch / multi-touch)?
- How do the colors appear?
- Update: Maybe a banchmark with this library can be useful? https://www.tldraw.com/

If you have an e-paper tablet, it would be great, if you could maybe add a video and have a test?

Thank you and best regards
Thomas

PS: I developed an integration for the note-taking app trilium, and therefore I am wondering, if the performance would be enough to use it via chrome browser on the tablet

title: Nested Collections
body:
I absolutely love excalidraw. I tell everyone about it. 

Recently, I upgraded to excalidraw plus because I was just drawing EVERYTHING into a single scene. It's a beautiful monster. I also had a bit of a scare of losing my work... yay cloud!

Anyways, I'm a really heavy user and organizing my sh*t is something I need in order to keep myself sane. As a solo founder there are so many damn moving pieces, especially considering that we are a deep tech startup. 

I'm making this post as a feature request to allow Excaldraw+ users the ability to Nest Collections. Or create folders, and then put collections inside the folders, or whatever you want to call them. 

here's a screen shot of a current collection that I'll be breaking out into multiple collections soon... but the problem ill have soon is having a HUGE list of collections... would be great if I could organize the collections and give the "parent" collection a name :) 
![Screen Shot 2022-06-06 at 3 02 16 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/17133963/172257115-ad2ff768-3480-4311-9a81-173354dd5b84.png)

Keep up the awesome work & thank you for the consideration!

title: Option for Isometric Grid
body:
I wasn't sure where I could put in suggestions or requests for Excalidraw, so I came to the github repo. 

I want to ask if it is feasible to add the option to add an isometric grid (like in Inkscape) to Excalidraw. One workaround I have been using is by creating a low opacity grid myself, like so: 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/82528318/174985006-8ea7e46a-a5fe-4808-814b-b90cd6d4b8d6.png)

But it quickly takes up a lot of the space on the board, slowing down the response time. 

Usually I use it to draw a quick design of a room or place I use in my artistic creations, like stories. It is useful to have a visual guide to write the story better. I could use Inkscape for the drawings, but sometimes I want to create some quick 3d models for the rooms and such. 

The hand drawn feel of Excalidraw is much useful to sell the e-stories I write. 

I would do a pull request myself, but I have no experience with Typescript yet.

Like pinkish icon in the last row, It acts exactly like the [paint-format](https://support.google.com/docs/thread/7467995/how-do-you-use-paint-format?hl=en) in doc (e.g. google doc) It copies all the data (Stroke color, Background Color, fill style, opacity, ...) and there is future possibility to paste only these styles to the selected item or multiple items (possibly via shortcuts)

title: For the love of God : introduce mouse mode for zooming and moving the canvas
body:
As with all apps that use a large amount of space to add and place objects, the brain thinks about them as a sort of a map. 
and to navigate a map, there is nothing simpler than : 
- scroll wheel for zoom in / out 
- drag with right button to move the canva

Why on earth do we need to press a modifier key for this behavior to take place ? when in reality it's the better and more useful way. 

99% of the time that I want to use the scroll wheel, what I what to do is change the scale in order to explore other areas of the canvas, not to move it up and down. the current way of doing things moves the canvas up and down which is rarely what the user wants. also with the current way of doing things the scroll wheel cannot be used to move the canva horizontally... 

Google maps uses the scroll wheel to zoom/in and out. it would suck if it was using it to move the map slightly up or down, and it would take 100 times more effort to explore another place of the map if it worked that way. 

Miro uses scroll wheel for zoom in and out. 

Even the graph view in obsidian works this way. (scroll wheel for zoom)

This is how it is done in all apps that know a thing or two about user friendly interfaces. 

So I have no idea why excalidraw has to be the way it is. At the very least you can offer a trackpad mode (the current one) and a mouse mode (with the changes that I proposed at the beginning). 

I quit using a mindmap app called ithoughts for this exact reason. I would have HUUUGE maps, and when I wanted to explore them the scrolling wheel just moves the entire mind map slightly up or slightly down instead of allowing me to quickly change perspective. It was perfect in all other ways. But the navigation part was a dealbreaker.

title: Feature request for Excalidraw
body:
First of all, I would like to thank the creator and community for their wonderful work with excalidraw, truly a phenomenal program for visual thinkers.

Features I would love to see in future updates:

1. Sticky notes, that popup when hovering or clicking on an image. The sticky notes should have the ability to write text, insert emoji’s and hyperlink them. 
-Hovering or clicking to popup the sticky note should be customizable in options
-Colors of Sticky notes, should be customizable as well.

* **Ideally** if possible, would be the ability to take an image (created or external) and turn it into a popup of another image on the white board. This would be useful in a host of different applications, like dashboard development, visual learning and structured data representation. One example, imagine having an organizational chart, where upon clicking or hovering on a job position, a photo of an employee would appear with their name. Now imagine, that name or photo being made into a hyperlink, which upon clicking would direct you towards an obsidian note which contains the employees bio or resume. 
 
2. Iframe for excalidraw to embedding videos, sound/voice-recordings, maps etc…

3. Able to paste gifs images on the white board, with the option to have them play/animate upon hovering, clicking or continuously play/animate.

For me, excalidraw is already a program that I value a lot. Should the above features one day be implemented it would make it legendary.

title: Simple example on how to use Excalidraw in standalone (html/js) with Backend on PHP Server
body:
Hello,

Here is a simple exemple of a standalone use of Excalidraw in html/js with backend for a php server.

- At start, Excali is loaded with your custom library file, that should be named "libray.excalidrawlib" in the same folder as the script.

- The main menu is changed to save & export on the server (instead of on the device).

- It allows to save jsonfile.excalidraw or export png file with embbeded scene metadata, that allows to re-edit the scene later.

- Add also some custom configuration of Excalidraw UI for example.


**Usage:** 

Rename the "index.txt" in "index.php" and put this file in a folder of your server's document root, then call it from client with the syntax:

`https://myhostname/pathto/index.php`

or to re-edit a previous file, use query string:  ?name=filename  (file should be in the same folder as php script)

`https://myhostname/pathto/index.php?name=image.png (or jsonfile.excalidraw)`

The files are saved in the script directory to simplify this example.

For a png image, Excalidraw doesn't load the image itself, but reload the complete Scene, extracting it from the metadata embedded inside, so you can continue to modify it again.
 
[index.txt](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/files/11423744/index.txt)
author: gitubbi

title: How do you compile standalone excalidraw with webpack ?

title: Are deleted strokes useful ?
body:
Hi, when I opened an Excalidraw drawing in md (using Obsidian, I don't know if the info is relevent), I saw that all the deleted strokes were still present, they are juste flagged "isDeleted : true" in the json object. I thougth it was for undo, but once you close the file you can't undo what you did before. I also tried deleting the flagged files during editing, and I could undo. That's why I think it's actually useless. Does it have a practical use ? Does other similar software do the same ?

Because of a lack of time at the moment I didn't do a lot of testing, but I tried prompting gpt for a quick script that deletes the elements with the attribute "isDeleted : true". I found that on small test files with a few strokes the file size decreases, but when I tried on a big file (a real note from class), the size actually increased. The code seems inefficient, but as I said, I don't have time to check in details rn. 

My idea is a script that deletes the deleted strokes from the json object, thus saving space. I wanted to ask you guys so I can understand all this better for when I have time to work on this idea. Thanks a lot and sorry if it's a stupid question, I'm not used to open source, it's my first time contributing.

title: Accessing Excalidraw icons when making a custom main menu
body:
In `@excalidraw/excalidraw==0.16.1`, there's a hacky way to access some of the icons.

For example: I can access the GitHub icon by going through the `MainMenu.DefaultItems.Socials`:

```javascript
import { Excalidraw } from "@excalidraw/excalidraw";
import { MainMenu } from "@excalidraw/excalidraw";

export default function App() {
    return (
        <Excalidraw>
            <MainMenu>
                <MainMenu.Group title="links">
                    <MainMenu.ItemLink
                        icon={MainMenu.DefaultItems.Socials().props.children[0].props.icon }
                        href="https://github.com/"
                        aria-label="GitHub"
                    >
                        GitHub
                    </MainMenu.ItemLink>
                </MainMenu.Group>
            </MainMenu>
        </Excalidraw>
    );
}
```

... which renders:

![the excalidraw hamburger menu with a GitHub icon in it](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/11916674/811e16d2-a0fe-42e5-8723-28e3b3be4996)

The `GithubIcon` constant is in `src/components/icons.tsx`, but these do not appear to be exported by default.

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/71ad3c53563fb6ffcc0e18a5157ca81e8c5698af/src/components/icons.tsx#L473-L479

Is there a better way to access these that I'm missing?

author: p2gdeveloper

title: Usecase: Annotating and discussing papers or sections of books
body:
In this thread, I'll describe a workflow for a specific usecase of Excalidraw, namely that of talking about a paper. Excalidraw is a great communication tool for this purpose.

I've been using it with a friend to discuss some papers. We typically take some screenshot images of certain parts of a paper which we then upload to a canvas.
Then we do the following:
- we write on top of the images
- we point arrows at specific content or regions in the images and annotate them with additional comments
- we highlight parts of the images (especially definitions and various identities)
- we annotate with text or make small doodles
- we draw some arrows between existing diagrams in the images to some portion of text

This develops over the course of the conversation in a free-form way. It would definitely be nice to have MathJax Support #5890 and of course if #7161 CRDTs were added those would be a great addition too.

Another thing which would be interesting is uploading a PDF, have Excalidraw split it into pages and convert the pages to images and pre-populate the Canvas with them, because currently that's what we do manually. 

It would also be nice to be able to make some small experiments while we read the paper. Of course we can open a separate Jupyter and start writing some snippet there. If Excalidraw offered a way to insert an editable piece of code just like Jupyter Cell, that we can move around and fold/unfold, that would also be amazing. The part that Excalidraw would have to do in this case would be to just send the code off to a Jupyter Notebook/Lab or to a Sagemath instance.
author: wsdookadr

title: Implement Live collaboration on top of <Excalidraw />
body:
Hello team,

I'm currently integrating `<Excalidraw />` into my project, and its UI customization capabilities aligns perfectly with our requirements. However, i'm facing difficulties incorporating the collaboration mode, a crucial feature for our project as it revolves around a collaborative learning platform.

I looked up the [*Collaboration mode - Self-hosting vs Collab on top of `<Excalidraw />`*](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3879) #3879 discussion and the [answer](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/3879#discussioncomment-1110524) by Aakansha and reproduced the following steps:
- Clone [excalidraw-room](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw-room)  and host the server myself 
- Clone [excalidraw](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw)
  -  Update the [entry](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/webpack.prod.config.js#L12) in *webpack.prod.config.js* to ["../../../excalidraw-app/index.tsx"](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/excalidraw-app/index.tsx)
- Release the npm package: execute `yarn build:umd` and then `yarn publish` 
 
I have no problems through these steps, but when i try to import the package and use the [`<ExcalidrawApp />`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/excalidraw-app/index.tsx#L801), it turns out that it can't be found.

Am i overlooking something or is there a step I might have missed?

Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

title: Is the excalidraw plus sidebar included in this repo?
body:
I have some gripes with the current functionality that i know won't be addressed, so i would like to clone the repo and make myself a custom build that fixes them. However i would also like to use the collections and scene management that excalidraw+ provides.
Is this possible, or do i need to choose one-or-the-other?
<!--
I would like to do this because of a combination of two reasons

1. I am currently trying out the trial and the collections & scene management is proving quite convenient. The functionality seems generally worth the price and i would like to continue using it

2. I am frustrated with the current dark mode, I use dark modes for contrast, because i find that i can distinguish colors much better in dark mode. However, excalidraw's dark mode makes some colors inaccessible (most notably bright yellow), washes out many others, and makes selecting a custom color (from the limited color space that remains) exceptionally difficult (literally 30+ minutes of work looking up the CSS style, looking up what formulas that corresponds to, then having apply that in reverse)
It also changes my images every time i paste, which is mildly annoying if i want to cut out a part of a pasted screenshot by screenshotting it again. And also just because every time i paste in some colorful UI it looks visibly washed out

Because of this, i was considering creating a custom build for myself that disables the CSS rules and either inverts the colors directly when theme is toggled, or doesn't do it at all 
But if i do this, can i still use excalidraw plus with it somehow, or am i stuck having to choose one or the other
-->

title: Using Excalidraw React build in a vanilla JS application
body:
Hi, I'm using the build of Excalidraw from React in a vanilla js web application with websocket.

Ideally, I'm using the WebSocket to enable real-time collaboration between many users.

**Problem:**  Canvas doesn't sync together with the same elements.

I'm using the same websocket implementation for a chat feature which works well, so not a websocket error.

I also tried saving the data to a useref to mimic external communication and restoring on the React app before building and it worked.

I can confirm the data is sending and receiving on both ends but for some reasons, it doesn't update the canvas with current elements on each user screen canvas connected.

An example code of my react app can be found in a  [codesandbox example](https://codesandbox.io/s/favourz-excalidraw-app-2w55td?file=/src/App.js) with detailed comments


Also, an overview of my websocket and vanilla js implementation can be found in a [codesnadbox example](https://codesandbox.io/s/favourz-excalidraw-app-2w55td?file=/external-index.html) with detailed comments.

I also commented on other methods I tried in the files.

Please help me resolve this as it's an urgent issue for a product I'm building.

cc: @ad1992 & @dwelle

title: Option to add title to svg to make key words searchable in GitHub

- [box_without_title.excalidraw.svg](https://github.com/SoumayaMauthoorMOJ/test-excalidraw/blob/main/box_without_title.excalidraw.svg) contains the same box but no title

[Searching for the word "Presentations" in GitHub](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3ASoumayaMauthoorMOJ%2Ftest-excalidraw%20Presentations&type=code) only returns a match if included in the title tag.

Can I raise a feature to have the option to add title (and perhaps description) tags to images to make svg images easier to search?

title: Add images to libraries
body:
Hi All - We just installed Excalidraw on Vercel via github and its working correctly - we wanted  to add pngs or svgs to our personal libaries - we know that Excalidraw does not support adding images  in the public  library yet, and the converter isn’t working properly but the feedback we got was  was that we could "modify the code to allow image embedding." 

So we spent the last 1.5 days reviewing the  excalidraw codebase to see how  we could do this for our fork  and tried lots of options including  writing a python script to work as an external tool that converts svg to excalidraw - even thought the conversion is successful, excalidraw still can't accept it as a  library file. We tried other things like changing file extension types but no success and
 we have now run out of ideas. 
However, if anyone has already done this or has suggestions on how we can do it please let us know. We are react / python devs and if a solution is successful we will happily share the solution code with the community

thanks, Garrett

title: Set up excalidraw and excalidraw-room on an EC2 instance and proxy the app using Cloudflare
body: To set up excalidraw-room on an EC2 instance and proxy the app using Cloudflare, you need to: 1- Cloudflare doesn’t support all ports. I was running the Excalidraw collaboration app on a port not supported by Cloudflare, as we’re using CF as a proxy. I have now changed this to a port supported https://developers.cloudflare.com/fundamentals/reference/network-ports/. 2: Most importantly, Cloudflare certificates only support the top-level domain. I am running another Node app on the same EC2 instance but on a different port: XXXX. The URL is https://api.shorterloop.com:XXXX/. For this, I needed to generate different certificates. 3: We needed to set up our own Firebase account, we did that! Posting this to help others in the community! [Our whiteboard](https://app.shorterloop.com):

title: How to use one domain for both excalidraw and excalidraw-room?
body:
This is one fantastic web app, I'm making much use of it.

That said, I'm stuck in setting up an Nginx reverse proxy -> docker stack where I use only one host domain name, e.g. excalidraw.example.com.

It's simple enough
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/060f88bd-a471-488b-9a6c-e55fea3fdf63)

I'd like for visitors to hit https://excalidraw.example.com to access Excalidraw. But, I'd also like visitors to be able to use the collaboration feature.
I have set up a docker-compose stack that looks like this:

```
version: "3.8"

services:
  excalidraw:
    build:
      context: ./excalidraw
      args:
        - NODE_ENV=production
    container_name: excalidraw
    ports:
      - "3333:80"
    restart: on-failure
    stdin_open: true
    healthcheck:
      disable: true
    volumes:
      - ./:/opt/node_app/app:delegated
      - ./package.json:/opt/node_app/package.json
      - ./yarn.lock:/opt/node_app/yarn.lock
      - notused:/opt/node_app/app/node_modules
    
  excalidraw-room:
    build:
      context: ./excalidraw-room
    environment:
      # Collaboration WebSocket
      PORT: 3002
    ports:
      - "4444:3002"

volumes:
  notused:
```

This is fairly standard configuration as far as I've been able to gather.

The Nginx revers-proxy looks like the following:

```
server {
    server_name excalidraw.example.com;
    ...
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://192.168.1.10:3333;
    }

    location /ws/ {
        # Web sockets
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_pass http://192.168.1.10:4444/;
        #return 200 'gangnam style!';
    }
```

The .env.production-file for Excalidraw looks like the following:

```
...
# socket server URL used for collaboration
VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=https://excalidraw.example.com/ws
...
```

When starting a collaboration session with this setup, there's an error in the output in the console log

```
wss://excalidraw.example.com/socket.io/?EIO=4&transport=websocket
```

As you can see, the log states `wss://excalidraw.example.com/` but it's totally omitting the "/ws" that I have put in .env.production.

I have only been able to solve this situation by creating another subdomain, e.g. "excalidraw-room.example.com" and point `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL` to that, but I'd much rather have one host delivering both services.

I can't for the life of me figure out why this is an issue. I'm just finding it hard to believe this isn't a thing that can be set up this way (without e.g. recoding large parts of the app).

title: how to know whether `AppState` is changed using `onPointerUp` API
body:
I have an idea to record the state, like exporting png, of excalidraw as `undo` and `redo` histories do. After capturing a series of png, splice them all to get a video.

[`onChange`](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/excalidraw-api#onchange) does not meet my request because moving a component will trigger the callback, which is unexpected. I just want to record the result of a moving action instead of details in the moving process. So I find [`onPointerUp`](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/excalidraw-api#onpointerup). When a pointerup event is triggered, it's quite likely that an action has been completed. In such a case, I can record the state by exporting it as a png or in some other format. However, since there's only a certain probability that an action will actually be executed, I need to determine whether the state has truly changed.

I want to judge whether `AppState` is changed when the callback function of [`onPointerUp`](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/excalidraw-api#onpointerup) is called. But I'm overwhelmed by too many APIs. How to judge whether the `AppState` is changed? Thanks a lot.

> `AppStateChange.calculate` might be a good choice but it seems that I need to store old `AppState`, which might consume large memory and not so efficient I think.

I find [the comment](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/8721#discussioncomment-11086139) helpful. But [excalidraw-animate](https://github.com/dai-shi/excalidraw-animate) is for animation, but not for recording I think.

title: Support for Symbols in AI-Generated Text-to-Diagram in Excalidraw
body:
🚀 Proposal: Support for Symbols in AI-Generated Text-to-Diagram in Excalidraw  

#### **Description:**  
Hello Excalidraw team and community,  

I’d like to propose adding **support for symbols** from Excalidraw’s vast library to enhance **text-to-AI generation** capabilities. This feature would allow users to reference existing symbols when generating diagrams via AI, making it easier to create structured, detailed, and professional-looking drawings.  

### **Problem Statement:**  
Currently, when using AI-generated text-to-diagram functionality, there’s **no direct way to reference Excalidraw’s existing symbols**. Users might need to manually insert icons or elements later, leading to inefficiencies.  

### **Proposed Solution:**  
- Enable users to specify **symbol names** in text descriptions (e.g., "Add a database icon").  
- Improve **symbol searchability** within AI-generated drawings.  

### **Potential Benefits:**  
✔️ **Faster workflow**: Users won’t need to manually adjust symbols.  
✔️ **More accurate AI diagrams**: Ensures AI-generated content aligns with standard notation.  
✔️ **Improved usability**: Makes text-to-diagram feature more intuitive.  

Would love to hear thoughts from the community and maintainers! 🚀  

Thanks,  
_Hossein_

title: Excalidraw with configurable storage for a fully self-hosted solution
body:
Hi All,

Just wanted to post about a POC I have been working on for another project that uses excalidraw as a dependency. I have created an excalidraw wrapper that allows configuration of any storage/document store backend for collboration/persistence. This helps replace firebase with a backend of choice.

I am sure there are many other implementations that do the same, but I wanted to build something configurable that doesn't require creating a fork and modifying the excalidraw code itself.

This is just a POC for now, to see if it works, there would some more clean-up/refactoring/testing required for it to be ready for production use, but just wanted to present this as an idea. Hopefully the core project itself could implement something similar.

The project can be found here: https://github.com/beltebelt/excalidraw
I have also created some examples using [pocketbase](https://github.com/beltebelt/excalidraw-with-storage-examples/blob/main/src/examples/pocketbase/WithPocketbase.tsx), [supabase](https://github.com/beltebelt/excalidraw-with-storage-examples/blob/main/src/examples/supabase/WithSupabase.tsx) and the original [firebase](https://github.com/beltebelt/excalidraw-with-storage-examples/blob/main/src/examples/firebase/WithFirebase.tsx)

Would love to hear your thoughts.

author: rjax

title: How about an Element Extension System?
body:
The whole thing started because my boss wanted to develop a UML diagramming system based on Excalidraw. You know, UML roughly consists of 14 major categories and 45 types of elements, so I thought I should start by implementing the simplest one.  

I decided to try writing an element called "Class" based on Excalidraw’s source code. Then I found that I had to modify 14 files, most of which were just to refine some type definitions to prevent TypeScript from reporting errors. The most critical parts—drawing the element and determining overlapping regions—weren't actually that much.

Moreover, these type definitions that needed modification were scattered across various code files, some even in language configuration files used for internationalization.  

Honestly, developing 45 elements this way would be quite frustrating.

The current approach requires extensive modifications across many files in the codebase just to extend custom elements in Excalidraw. This leads to two major issues:  

1. Modifying these files is cumbersome—you have to tweak them bit by bit and carefully debug where the type definitions are broken and need fixing. This process is not intuitive.  
2. Projects that rely on Excalidraw for secondary development will find it nearly impossible to upgrade to newer versions. Since you've made extensive modifications to the source code, merging future updates from Excalidraw would be a nightmare.

Guys, shouldn't we think of a way to improve this situation?

ps: the modified codes are in the attachments

[excalidraw.patch](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19204449/excalidraw.patch)

author: rjax

title: Feature Request: Layer/Element Visibility Toggle Within Frames
body:
I'd like to propose adding a display/hide functionality for elements within the same frame. This would enable users to create interactive diagrams with toggleable layers of information, all contained within a unified frame structure.

## Use Case

As someone who creates technical diagrams and presentations within Excalidraw, I often need to show different states or layers of information in the same spatial context. Currently, this requires:
- Creating duplicate frames
- Copying elements between frames
- Ensuring perfect alignment

A visibility toggle would simplify this workflow tremendously, allowing:
- Single frame maintenance
- Toggle between different element combinations
- Creation of interactive diagrams for presentations

## Implementation Suggestion

1. Add a "visibility" property to elements
2. Create a sidebar panel for managing visibility groups/layers 
3. Implement keyboard shortcuts for quick toggling
4. Allow exporting with visibility states as separate images/slides

## Benefits

- **Better Presentations**: Create step-by-step reveals without file complexity
- **Enhanced Interactivity**: Enable richer diagrams for educational/presentation purposes

## Questions for the Community

- Would this feature be valuable to your workflows?
- Any additional use cases I'm not considering?
- Preferences on UI implementation for managing visibility?
- How would this interact with existing features like embed/link frames?

I'm happy to elaborate further or contribute to discussions on implementation approach if there's interest!

New to github, so forgive me if this issue has been brought up before.  I did do a search, but found nothing.

I'm using excalidraw to create a personal website, specifically a massive family tree with hundreds of names.    I've been able to use the Share - Embed link to create a read-only version with a start position to essentially become that website.  This part works great.

I had hopes for using the Link to Target to create intra site links to be able to jump around the white board, come back to Home view etc.  The problem is the Link to Target is not setup to be used in read only mode.     From read only "website" mode,  the links don't smoothly transition, instead they open the link in a new tab.   Worse, the new tab is no longer read-only.

Does anyone have a workaround for this?   
Am i just S.O.L because I'm trying to use excalidraw in a way it wasn't meant to be used?   
Being new to github, can this issue be raised to the developers for consideration to be used in this way -  to create link to targets that work and keep you in read-only mode.
Additionally,  the physical target link, that little blue arrow, is really really small.  I'd like the option for setting that size.

Thanks.
author: highway-six

title: Option for emoji insertion in more tools section.
body: Hey there, First of all, my gratitude to the wonderful maintainers and the community behind this amazing tool! I have a small and straight forward feature request: I would like to have an option to **select emojis from the toolbar**. I use Excalidraw in the browser, and every time I want to insert an emoji I have to Google it — copy the emoji and paste it onto the board. This is annoying and inconvenient. Native support is would be greate! I'm opening this discussion to take opinions, before opening an issue or PR. Thank you.
author: pradeept

title: [Idea/Feature] Excalidraw as rendering engine for mermaid diagrams?
body:
I've found writing [mermaid diagrams](https://docs.mermaidchart.com/mermaid-oss/intro/index.html) to be powerful, especially when working with LLMs. The text based format makes them convenient to include in markdown files for documentation. And several common tools like Obsidian, VSCode, and GitHub natively render mermaid diagram code blocks.

However I really like the Excalidraw style, and the default mermaid rendering style with crisp lines and sharp edges can feel awkward for so many diagrams. I would love to be able to use Excalidraw or some subset library from it as the rendering engine for mermaid diagrams. 

I see the [excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw project](https://github.com/excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw) that exists, but my understanding is that its focus is on converting mermaid diagrams into full featured, editable drawings that can be worked with in excalidraw.com. 

Would it be feasible to create an Excalidraw rendering engine library that could render Mermaid diagrams in the Excalidraw style? What would it take to make this happen?
author: Graham42

title: [Feature Request] Textbox Resizing Behavior Improvement
body: When resizing a **textbox**, the text should **not scale down**. Instead, the box should introduce **line breaks** automatically so the font size stays consistent and you don't have to manually type line breaks.

title: [Feature request] Button on Toolbar (or sticky always onscreen) for quick jump to linked object
body: Hello! I couldn't find this already requested, apologies in advance if it was. Since one excalidraw image tells more than 1000 words: It doesn't have to be button on toolbar, any object / group etc. that could set to: sticky, always on top, always on screen (special canvas layer?) in a fixed position..

author: simonausten

title: Built a Durable Object backend for real time Excalidraw sync
body:
I wanted to see how hard this would be. Turns out… not hard at all.
Simple conflict resolution, a single Durable Object for coordination, and suddenly you have real-time collaboration that scales to absurd levels for a fraction of the cost of a socket.io setup.

What you get:

sub-second sync

cursor presence

full history

effortless horizontal scale

sockets that hibernate when idle

Package
https://www.npmjs.com/package/@shvm/excalidraw-live-sync


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19985843-8463-4f10-86cb-21dd093230df


Also checkout liva.shvm.in
author: shivamd20

title: Embedded frames
body:
Why not allow the embedding of one frame within another?
So that when you move one frame, the embedded frames move as well.

[DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/excalidraw/excalidraw) mentioned that is designed this way because:
- keep a simple and predictable structure
- avoid complex rendering and selection behaviors
- avoid infinite nesting
- the frames are designed for organizing content within a single level of containment

Any more ideas?

Having said all this, it makes sense to consider implementing this feature, or this behavior will never be incorporated?

title: excalidraw没有开启新画布的功能吗？

author: zsdili

title: How do you manage to track movements outside the screen?
body:
Hello, I noticed that user can grab the screen with panning tool (H) and it will work even if you go outside the browser window. Usual events like `pointermove`, up do my knowledge, don't work when pointer is moved outside the screen. The only exception I know to this is the [Pointer Lock API], but excalidraw doesn't use it. 

[Pointer Lock API]: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Pointer_Lock_API

There is no input field for width and height and I cannot even see this displayed anywhere.

title: This is an updated standalone version of Excalidraw for PHP that you can integrate into your web application using HTML/JavaScript with a PHP backend.
body:
Based on discussion thread:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/discussions/6569

I copied the main implementation from this discussion page and added some functions and enhancements. Please feel free to improve or update it, as I know there are still areas where the code can be simplified and optimized.

This version includes additional enhancements and usability improvements, particularly for mobile devices and drawing workflow efficiency.

Repository link: https://github.com/LDSmj/excalidraw_standalone

## ✨ Features

### 🖊 Enhanced Pen Tool (Multi-Mode)

The Pen tool supports multiple drawing behaviors using consecutive clicks:

- **First Click**
    - Enables auto-correct mode
    - Draw straight lines
    - Draw multi-point lines
    - Automatic circle detection

- **Second Click**
    - Activates Highlighter mode
    - Includes auto-correct support

- **Third Click**
    - Activates Free Draw mode

This reduces the need to manually switch tools and improves drawing efficiency.

---

### 📱 Improved Mobile Experience

In the default Excalidraw web version, double-tapping activates the **Add Text** tool.

On mobile devices, this can cause accidental text insertion when:
- Moving elements
- Adjusting shapes
- Interacting with the canvas

To improve usability, the double-tap-to-add-text feature has been disabled in mobile view.

## 🏗 Tech Stack

- Frontend: HTML + JavaScript (ES Modules / CDN or self-hosted)
- Backend: PHP
- Architecture: Standalone integration-ready setup

## 🤝 Contributing

Contributions, improvements, and refactoring suggestions are welcome.
Feel free to open an issue or submit a pull request.
author: LDSmj

title: Excalidraw Plus MCP Alpha Feedback: 39/39 Tests Passed, 4 Feature Requests
body:
## Context

I received alpha API access to the Excalidraw Plus MCP today and ran a comprehensive test suite across multiple AI coding assistants (Claude Code and Gemini CLI) from multiple projects simultaneously.

**Testing environment:** Claude Code v2.1.84, Gemini CLI v0.36.0, macOS Tahoe 26.4
**API key permissions:** Full (all 26 tools accessible)
**Tests run:** 39 across 3 terminals, 2 projects, 2 AI platforms
**Result:** 39/39 passed. Zero failures.

## Overall Assessment

The MCP worked flawlessly during my initial testing. All 26 tools responded correctly across 39 test cases run from 3 terminals (2 Claude Code sessions, 1 Gemini CLI), 2 different projects, and both the open-source and Plus MCPs simultaneously. Zero failures. Clean error handling. Fast response times.

This performs well for alpha software.

## What Worked Well

1. **Instant API key provisioning.** Requested access, received key the same day. No forms, no waiting.
2. **Clean error responses.** Invalid scene IDs return `{"error":"Scene not found"}` — no crashes, no stack traces, no ambiguity.
3. **Complete CRUD coverage.** All 26 tools work as documented. Scene content read/write, collection management, workspace management, invites, logs — everything tested and functional.
4. **Concurrent access from multiple clients.** Two Claude Code sessions and one Gemini CLI session accessed the workspace simultaneously without conflicts.
5. **Streamable HTTP transport.** Once configured correctly (`"type": "http"` in Claude Code's `.mcp.json`), the connection held up across all 39 test cases without a single drop.

## Feature Requests / Suggestions

### 1. Add `createdBy` or `source` field to scene metadata

**Current behavior:** API-created scenes have `creator: null`. Mobile-created and web-created scenes may show a user ID, but there's no way to distinguish which client created a scene.

**Requested:** A `source` or `createdBy` field that records the creation method (e.g., "api", "web", "ios", "android"). This would help users who access their workspace from multiple devices and AI agents to understand where a drawing originated.

**Use case:** I access Excalidraw from iPad, iPhone, web, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI. When I list scenes, I want to know which ones I sketched on my iPad vs. which ones an AI agent generated.

### 2. PATCH-style partial update for scene content

**Current behavior:** `update_scene_content` performs a complete replacement. Any elements not included in the PUT body are deleted.

**Requested:** A PATCH-style endpoint that allows adding, modifying, or removing individual elements without sending the entire array. For large scenes with 50+ elements, the complete replacement approach is:
- Bandwidth-heavy (full JSON roundtrip)
- Error-prone (if the GET→modify→PUT cycle races with another client)
- Token-expensive for AI agents (sending 50+ elements as JSON in a single tool call)

**Suggested API:**
```json
PATCH /scenes/{id}/content
{
  "add": [],
  "update": [],
  "remove": ["element-id-1", "element-id-2"]
}
```

### 3. Document the `"type": "http"` requirement for Claude Code

Claude Code's `.mcp.json` schema requires `"type": "http"` alongside `"url"` and `"headers"` for HTTP-based MCP servers. Without the `type` field, the config fails schema validation silently (no error in chat, tools just don't appear).

This took about 20 minutes of documentation review and troubleshooting to figure out. Adding a note to your MCP docs page about the `type` field requirement for Claude Code would save other users that friction.

Filed as issues in both repos:
- excalidraw/excalidraw-mcp#64
- excalidraw/excalidraw#11107

### 4. Scene version conflict detection

`update_scene_content` accepts a `sceneVersion` parameter, but it's unclear whether this is used for optimistic concurrency control (reject update if version mismatch) or just metadata. Clear documentation on this would help, especially for AI agents that may race with human edits on the same scene.

## Minor Observations

- The `label` shorthand from the open-source Excalidraw MCP (e.g., `"label": {"text": "Node"}` on a rectangle) is not supported by the Plus API, which requires the standard Excalidraw format (separate text elements with `containerId`/`boundElements`). Expected behavior (different APIs), but worth noting for users building OSS→Plus pipelines.

- Soft-delete behavior on `delete_scene` and `delete_collection` works correctly. Would be helpful to document the trash retention period.

## Use Case

I'm a PhD candidate researching LLM-based extraction from FAA drone sighting reports. I use Excalidraw for concept sketching, architecture diagrams, and presentation visuals across Obsidian (desktop), iPad, and AI coding assistants. The Plus MCP enables a workflow where I sketch on my iPad, then Claude Code or Gemini CLI can read, modify, and push diagrams back to the cloud — bridging mobile brainstorming with AI-assisted refinement.

## Environment

| Component | Version |
|-----------|---------|
| Claude Code | 2.1.84 (Homebrew) |
| Gemini CLI | 0.36.0 |
| macOS | Tahoe 26.4 |
| MCP transport | Streamable HTTP |
| API version | v3 (per workspace metadata) |

Available for follow-up testing, feature validation, or detailed bug reports.
author: mikehickey2

title: ExcaliMath — open-source plugin that adds LaTeX equations, function graphs, and STEM shape libraries to Excalidraw
body:
Hi everyone,

I've built ExcaliMath, an open-source companion plugin that extends Excalidraw with math and STEM education capabilities. It wraps @excalidraw/excalidraw non-destructively — no fork, just a React component you drop into renderTopRightUI.

**What it does**
Equation Layer — Type LaTeX, get rendered equations on the canvas. Uses KaTeX with MathML output so equations render correctly inside Excalidraw's data URL images. Includes a library of 40+ pre-built expressions. Click any equation to edit it.

**Graph Layer** — Plot functions like sin(x) or x^2 + 2*x using Plotly.js. Supports up to 5 functions per graph, configurable axes, CSV data import, and 7 preset templates. All evaluation goes through mathjs (no eval()). Graphs have transparent backgrounds by default so they blend with the canvas.

**Shape Libraries** — 80+ curriculum-aligned shapes across 6 STEM packs:

- Geometry (triangles, polygons, coordinate grids, protractor)
- Algebra (fraction bars, algebra tiles, Venn diagrams, function machine)
- Statistics (bar chart, pie chart, histogram, box plot frames)
- Physics/Circuits (30 components: resistors, capacitors, transistors, all 7 logic gates with proper SVG bezier curves, meters, diodes)
- Biology (cell diagrams, DNA helix, mitosis stages)
- Chemistry (Bohr atom, periodic table tile, bond types, lab equipment)

All shapes insert as native Excalidraw elements (grouped for easy movement) or SVG images (for smooth curves). Searchable browser with pack filtering and .excalidrawlib import.

**UI**
Single toolbar button opens a compact sidebar with icon tabs (∑ Equation, ƒ Graph, △ Shapes). Supports both dark and light themes — auto-detects from Excalidraw's appState.

**Integration**

<Excalidraw
  excalidrawAPI={setApi}
  renderTopRightUI={() =>
    api ? <ExcaliMath excalidrawAPI={api} theme="auto" /> : null
  }
/>

Also ships as a desktop app (Electrobun) that works fully offline.

Links

- GitHub: [https://github.com/tamerUAE/excalimath](url)
- License: MIT

Built for students, teachers, and content authors. Feedback and contributions welcome!

title: Unofficial Excalidraw Desktop
body:
Hey 👋 there, I really like Excalidraw and I often find it superior to similar tools.

I started working on an unofficial Excalidraw Desktop app/client some time ago. It started as an somewhat simple wrapper around the official Excalidraw library, nicely packaged as a Tauri application.

Online library browsing and intercepting the clicks to redirect to the Tauri app and install it there worked for some time, but I might have broken that during some refactoring. Manual download and install still works though.

Currently it aims to be fully "autonomous" and not simply wrap the Excalidraw library.

There are many quirks and workarounds which then needs other workarounds for these workarounds 🙈 (e.g. Linux/Gnome/Wayland/Mutter, where the WM Mutter reports false  dimensions for outer bounds with decorations, which keeps the app growing on every launch, which can be "fixed" by disabling decorations; but that opens up other issues 🙈).

In the small subset I tested, it works okayish for me, but I would also like to invite people to give it a whirl and maybe report bugs.

I plan to have "multiplayer" included as a direct p2p version which doesn't require you to have any account at all (similar to a tool I built some time ago, which also has real p2p: https://getbananas.net) in the next few weeks.

By "multiplayer" I mean optional opt-in sharing and collaboration on a canvas.

Maybe you want to give it a whirl: https://github.com/mistweaverco/excalidraw-desktop

(Yes, yes, a dedicated website is already on my list, but I'm focused on the app itself for the moment)
author: gorillamoe

title: [Request?] Zoning or user-edependent edit rights.
body:
So as a teacher, i love excalidraw. The only thing that sometimes irks me, is that everyone can edit everything. Yeah the bad apples can be found through the names, but sometimes it geniously happens by accident, which can be undone, but sometimes students are somewhat tech illiterate and it initially creates some chaos, especially with multiple students in one board.

So my Idea was either being able to create different Zones in one board that each have a "access-code". You can give these access codes to the students and they can only edit in that special zone; as a admin you can still edit everything. I guess this is complicated, since you would have to introduce roles and the whole DB behind that and stuff. 

Alternatively, (Guest-)Users can't edit stuff of other users. No accidental delete, at worst they write over the other stuff and can then easily erase what they wrote. Maybe easier? But i dunno if that would slow down performance since you would have to store and check what user added what?

I would honestly try myself with - pls don't kill me - vibecoding (since i made some good experience with local-only stuff without internet access), but i believe I would be dealing DB stuff and therefore security stuff and i am definetly not fucking with that with no expertise whatsoever. Also, I don't want to add unnecessary workload for validating bad commits to every amazing contributor .....

Maybe something like this was already in planning? I can't find anything that has stuff like that and it would make a lot of lessons way less stressful.

author: griseouslight

title: Table creator script.
body: I was watch this video [[AI Scripting Superpowers](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6BjhUyfS4iM&t=497s)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6BjhUyfS4iM&t=497s) and I decided to follow the steps and play with creating a few scripts. I thought I'd share it here in case any one else would like to use it. ### Table creation process Since manually creating a table in Excalidraw was tiresome, I created a script that would help with that process. The way it works is that you there is a popup where you enter the number of columns and rows that you would like to have, and then select where you want the headers of the table to be located. (if you want headers) And then on the next popup it has created a form for you to enter the contents of the table. ### Templates Since my use case for using tables is often conjugation or declension tables, the headings are often the same, and I wanted to be able to skip entering them each time. To do this I added a place where you could select a template to use on the first popup. To save a template that you want to use later you fill out the header information, on the second cell entry popup and click a save template button on that page. ### Limitations 1. I haven't testing this script with a mobile device, only on a laptop. 2. I wanted the headers to be a different color than the regular cells so I hardcoded the color of the headers into the script. I'm using obsidian currently on a light theme, and the color I choose for the headers is simply black, if your using a dark theme then this isn't going to work for you, you should go and change what color you want the headers to be in the script. This is the line you would need to change ```js ea.style.strokeColor = headerVal ? "#000000" : (st.currentItemStrokeColor || "#000000"); ``` If you type "black" into the obsidian find function, you should be able to figure out where this line in the script is fairly easily. 3. There is only one style of table that I created. ### Disclaimer The script was created with ai, there might be bugs, and I don't have any intention of maintaining the script in any way. Because of the way it was created, the code would most likely need a lot of work on it before it could go into any script library. I'm not familiar enough with the excalidraw api or obsidians to do that work. --- This is just one of the scripts that I've created this way, I'll most likely post a few more. Here is the file for the script. [Create Table.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28616612/Create.Table.md)
author: biggyB

title: Bind a text view to a property.
body:
I'm sharing another script that I've made following the process from [AI Scripting Superpowers](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6BjhUyfS4iM&t=497s).  The purpose of this script it to make it possible to bind a text element to an obsidian property,  and then to update the text elements when the property changes.  This could be helpful in using Excalidraw as a dashboard.  

There are three different scripts to make this work.  The first one creates the bound element.
[Create Bound Text Element.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29223408/Create.Bound.Text.Element.md)

The second here,  is a script that you can run to update all of the bound elements.  
[Update Bound Text Elements.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29223422/Update.Bound.Text.Elements.md)

And finally,  this last script can be placed in the Startup script.  It will update the elements automatically,  when you load the page.  
[Start up script.md](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29223482/Start.up.script.md)

So to make the startup script work you need to set its location under the Excalidraw settings -> Excalidraw Automate -> Startup script.
author: biggyB

title: Excalidraw Agent
body: Hello, I've been using Excalidraw for over a year now, and it's been an incredibly helpful tool. Thank you for building and maintaining it! Lately, I've been doing a lot of reporting and documentation, and I wanted a more efficient way to create diagrams. That led me to integrate Excalidraw with my AI agent as a tool. I put together an open-source project here: https://github.com/amine0110/excalidraw-agent I wanted to share it with the community in case others find it useful as well. Once connected to your preferred LLM or AI coding assistant, it lets you generate and edit Excalidraw diagrams using natural language, making the whole workflow much faster. I hope it helps others be more productive. Feedback and contributions are always welcome!
